xBRIEFInfo.description: Scope xBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #59

plan.title: Break banners: "I did." button and per-type tallies

narratives.Description: Break banners: "I did." button and per-type tallies
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

Add an **"I did."** action on break notification banners and keep a running tally of completed breaks by type, with controls to clear tallies per category or all at once.

Break prompts today (`BreakBanner`, from #2 / PR #48) show the prompt message plus **Snooze** and **Dismiss** only. Break kinds are `stretch` / `walk` / `water` / `custom` (`src/lib/breaks.ts`). There is no completion acknowledgment or completion metrics UI yet.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Break notification banners include an **"I did."** button (alongside existing Snooze / Dismiss).
- [ ] Choosing **"I did."** records a completion for that break’s type and dismisses (or otherwise clears) the active banner appropriately.
- [ ] A running tally is shown for each break type that applies (`stretch`, `walk`, `water`, `custom` as used in the product).
- [ ] The user can clear **one** break category’s tally at a time via a control next to that metric.
- [ ] The user can clear **all** tallies at once via a single control.

## Open question

- Should tallies **persist across sessions** (e.g. local storage) unless specified otherwise? Confirm when implementing if not decided here.

## Context

- Related: #2, PR #48
- Current banner actions: Snooze + Dismiss only (`src/components/BreakBanner.tsx`)
narratives.Requirements:
FR-1: Break notification banners include an "I did." button alongside existing Snooze / Dismiss.
FR-2: Choosing "I did." records a completion for that break's type and dismisses (clears) the active banner.
FR-3: A running tally is shown for each break type (stretch, walk, water, custom).
FR-4: The user can clear one break category's tally via a control next to that metric.
FR-5: The user can clear all tallies at once via a single control.
NFR-1: Tallies persist in localStorage across sessions.
Out of scope: #61 banner stacking (single active banner is OK).
narratives.LockedDecisions: Refinement 2026-07-14 (#59 triage/implement, operator lock): (1) AC locked as FR-1–5 + NFR-1 from the issue body. (2) Persistence: tallies persist in localStorage across sessions (sensible default; closes the issue open question). (3) Tallies UI lives near BreakPanel settings/metrics area. (4) Out of scope: #61 stacking — single active banner is OK; do not invent unrelated scope.

A running tally is shown for each break type that applies (`stretch`, `walk`, `water`, `custom` as used in the product).
The user can clear **one** break category’s tally at a time via a control next to that metric.
